title: delete statement panics when joining a non-partitioned table with a partitioned table
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
create table t1(a int, b int);
create table t2(a int, b int) partition by hash(a) partitions 2;
insert into t1 value(1,1);
insert into t2 value(1,1);
delete t1, t2 from t1, t2 where t1.a = t2.a;
```


### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

Success.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
ERROR 1105 (HY000): runtime error: index out of range [5] with length 4
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

v8.5, latest master
poc: create table t1(a int, b int);
create table t2(a int, b int) partition by hash(a) partitions 2;
delete t1, t2 from t1, t2 where t1.a = t2.a;
key_bug_features: delete statement joining non-partitioned and partitioned tables
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3(c int, d varchar(50));
CREATE TABLE t4(c int, d varchar(50)) PARTITION BY RANGE (c) (PARTITION p0 VALUES LESS THAN (10), PARTITION p1 VALUES LESS THAN (20));
DELETE t3, t4 FROM t3 JOIN t4 ON t3.c = t4.c WHERE t3.c < 10;
